name: Ret20_0
description: Past 20-trading-day cumulative close-to-close return without skipping recent days.
source_pdf: 国信证券_金融工程专题研究：动量类因子全解析_【发现报告 fxbaogao.com】.pdf
selection_reason: Simple recent-return momentum is repeatedly used in the report and is directly computable from adjusted close prices.
investment_logic: As A-share institutional participation rises, recent price strength can persist instead of reversing in some stock universes and market states.
hypothesis: Stocks with stronger cumulative returns over the most recent 20 trading days will have higher short-term future returns.

variables.Close_t: Adjusted close price on date t
variables.Close_{t-20}: Adjusted close price 20 trading days before date t

implementation_plan: Use the adjusted close wide panel.
Compute close divided by close shifted by 20 trading days minus one.
Return the wide panel as standard long factor output.
window_reason: The factor compares current close with close 20 trading days earlier.
notes: Included as a baseline momentum factor, though the report notes traditional one-year momentum is weaker in the full A-share universe.

name: Ret240_20_RemoveUpLimit
description: One-year momentum skipping the most recent 20 trading days, with daily limit-up observations excluded from the compounded return.
source_pdf: 国信证券_金融工程专题研究：动量类因子全解析_【发现报告 fxbaogao.com】.pdf
selection_reason: The report highlights this as a major improvement over traditional momentum and it can be approximated from adjusted close returns.
investment_logic: A-share limit-up episodes can reflect temporary overreaction; excluding limit-up days leaves a cleaner persistent momentum signal.
hypothesis: Stocks with higher long-horizon cumulative returns after removing limit-up daily returns will have higher future returns.
formulation: Ret240_20_RemoveUpLimit = cumulative return over [T0-260, T0-20] after excluding limit-up daily returns

variables.limit_up_day: A day whose close-to-close return is approximately at or above the daily price limit

logic_expression: compound(return_ where return_ < limit_up_threshold over 240-day window ending 20 days ago)
implementation_plan: Compute daily close-to-close returns from adjusted close.
Mask likely limit-up observations, for example daily returns greater than or equal to 9.5%.
Shift returns by 20 trading days to skip the most recent month.
Compound the previous 240 valid daily returns with masked limit-up days excluded.
Return the compounded value as standard long factor output.
window_reason: The factor needs a 240-day formation window plus 20 skipped trading days.
viable_reason: Uses adjusted close; exact limit-up flags are unavailable, but a close-return threshold approximation is implementable.
notes: Exact涨停 identification would be better with limit price fields; current zer0factor version can approximate using close returns.

name: RankMom120_20
description: Path-aware momentum using the average cross-sectional rank of daily returns over a 120-day window, skipping the most recent 20 days.
source_pdf: 国信证券_金融工程专题研究：动量类因子全解析_【发现报告 fxbaogao.com】.pdf
selection_reason: The report identifies Rank momentum as a key path-momentum method and shows stronger performance for medium/long lookbacks.
investment_logic: Persistent daily relative strength is more informative than a return caused by a few extreme days.
hypothesis: Stocks with consistently higher daily return ranks over the formation window will have higher future returns.

variables.R_i,d: Daily return of stock i on day d
variables.rank_cs: Cross-sectional ascending rank by daily return on each date

implementation_plan: Compute daily close-to-close returns from adjusted close.
For each trade date, compute each stock's cross-sectional percentile or standardized rank of daily return.
Shift the rank panel by 20 trading days to skip the short-term reversal window.
Take a 120-day rolling average of the shifted rank panel.
Return the averaged rank momentum as standard long factor output.
window_reason: The factor needs a 120-day rank averaging window plus 20 skipped trading days.
viable_reason: Uses only adjusted close and cross-sectional operations.
notes: The report excludes new/ST/suspended stocks during ranking; current zer0factor data contract does not yet expose those filters.

name: Smooth240_1
description: Path smoothness momentum measured by displacement-to-path ratio over 240 trading days, skipping the most recent day.
source_pdf: 国信证券_金融工程专题研究：动量类因子全解析_【发现报告 fxbaogao.com】.pdf
selection_reason: It represents the report's path-momentum idea and is directly computable from daily returns.
investment_logic: For the same cumulative return, a smoother path contains less noise and better describes persistent price movement.
hypothesis: Stocks whose price movement is smoother over the formation window will show different future return behavior than stocks whose movement is jumpy.
formulation: Smooth240_1 = cumulative return over [T0-241, T0-1] / sum(abs(daily returns)) over [T0-241, T0-1]

variables.daily returns: Close-to-close daily returns in the formation window

implementation_plan: Compute daily close-to-close returns from adjusted close.
Shift returns by one trading day.
Compute 240-day cumulative return ending at T0-1.
Compute 240-day rolling sum of absolute shifted daily returns.
Divide cumulative return by absolute-return path length.
Return the smoothness ratio as standard long factor output.
window_reason: The 240-day window ending one day before T0 requires 241 days of close prices.
relevance_reason: Daily per-stock mathematical price path factor.
notes: The report finds this raw smoothness factor behaves more like a reversal signal in the full market, so expected_relation is set to lower_factor_predicts_higher_return.

name: OverNightMom20
source_pdf: 国信证券_金融工程专题研究：动量类因子全解析_【发现报告 fxbaogao.com】.pdf
selection_reason: The report highlights overnight momentum as a distinct short-horizon price component and it is computable from open and close.
investment_logic: Overnight price moves can capture information incorporated outside continuous trading hours and may persist over short horizons.
hypothesis: Stocks with stronger cumulative overnight returns over the past 20 trading days will have higher short-term future returns.

variables.Open_d: Adjusted open price on day d
variables.Close_{d-1}: Adjusted close price on the previous trading day

implementation_plan: Use adjusted open and adjusted close panels.
Compute daily overnight log return as log(open / previous close).
Take the 20-day rolling sum of overnight returns.
Return the rolling overnight momentum as standard long factor output.
window_reason: The rolling overnight sum uses 20 daily overnight returns and previous close values.
relevance_reason: Daily per-stock mathematical OHLC price factor.
viable_reason: Uses only adjusted open and close.
